sourceMovie.title: Inception
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dream heists, zero-gravity hallways, and a spinning top that broke group chats—Nolan’s blockbuster puzzle wears a suit and brings feelings under the spectacle.

recommendations.title: The Matrix
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Reality isn’t real—philosophy with kung fu and leather. If Inception’s rules-and-layers structure hooked you, this is the godfather.

recommendations.title: Shutter Island
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Same release-year mind games, different genre—psychological thriller framing that keeps rewriting what you think you know.

recommendations.title: Interstellar
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: More Nolan sci-fi with time as a character and emotion as fuel—bigger tears, bigger cosmos, same love of practical spectacle.

recommendations.title: TRON: Legacy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Digital worlds as architecture—thin script, absurdly gorgeous sound and visuals. Good if Inception’s ‘vibe-first’ midnight energy is your thing.

recommendations.title: Looper
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Time travel with noir grit and a nasty ethical question—tighter and messier than Inception, but the puzzle bones are there.

recommendations.title: The Prestige
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Nolan’s twisty obsession duel—less explosions, more sleight-of-hand. If you like structure as storytelling, this is peak.

recommendations.title: Coherence
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Micro-budget dinner party that fractures reality—improvised energy and escalating paranoia for puzzle fans who want indie scale.

recommendations.title: Edge of Tomorrow
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Video-game loop as blockbuster—propulsive, funny, and crystal-clear rules. Less dream logic, more repeatable mayhem.

recommendations.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Multiverse maximalism with emotional payoff—if Inception’s layers excited you, this turns the knob until it snaps off (in a good way).

recommendations.title: Minority Report
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spielberg sci-fi thriller with world-building and moral questions—sleek future noir for fans of rules-based futures.

faq.question: What movies are like Inception but easier to follow?
faq.answer: Edge of Tomorrow lays out its loop cleanly; Minority Report is procedural sci-fi with a strong through-line.

faq.question: Does the Inception top fall?
faq.answer: Nolan intentionally leaves ambiguity—the point is less the answer than how obsession and doubt linger after the cut to black.

faq.question: What should I watch for more Christopher Nolan heist energy?
faq.answer: The Dark Knight for urban scale, Tenet for time inversion, Dunkirk for structural experimentation across timelines.
